items.title: 'How do I survive this?' The financial burden of Catalina's cancer treatment

children.content: Research from the Cancer Council shows more than 38 per cent of cancer patients lose their income during treatment. For Catalina Alcazar, it was an unexpected extra burden as she faced treatment for a tumour in her oesophagus.

items.title: Woman dies as fire in north Canberra unit causes 'substantial damage'

children.content: Emergency services were called to the Wallcliffe Flats in Belconnen at about 6:30am where they found a ground-floor unit alight.

items.title: The dilemma Wallabies and All Blacks face when players head overseas

children.content: Both Australia and New Zealand strive to protect Super Rugby Pacific as their premier domestic competition, even it if it has consequences for the Wallabies and All Blacks.

items.title: A Canberra woman's husband said not to book return flights. It was a case of exit trafficking

children.content: Advocates for migrant women say they're seeing more cases of exit trafficking — where an offender uses coercion, threats or deception to organise a person's departure from Australia.

items.title: Natural burials offer families peace at NSW conservation cemetery

children.content: After nearly 10 years getting public health and council approvals, a natural burial ground on the NSW south coast begins letting people have a simpler, more environmentally friendly farewell.

items.title: Woman killed in bus crash asked to be organ donor just days earlier

children.content: Uqasha Imran was fatally injured when a bus driver struck her on her motorcycle in 2025. Now, her family are promoting the importance of organ donation while helping other young athletes like her.

items.title: The parts of Victoria adding the biggest share to disaster damage bills

children.content: At least $57 billion worth of infrastructure in Victoria is vulnerable to more frequent and severe bushfires, floods and extreme heat. This figure is expected to grow.

items.title: 'Thriller' history book about the bark petitions wins $50k literary prize

children.content: The 1963 Näku Dhäruk or Yirrkala bark petitions led to Australia's first land rights law. A new book about them just won two prizes at the NSW Literary Awards.

items.title: It's time for Eric Abetz to tackle Tasmania's debt. How far will he go?

items.title: Behind the front lines of the war against illegal waste

items.title: A secret mouse experiment in the 1990s threatened Brisbane's water supply

children.content: Brisbane was days away from running out of drinkable water in the 1990s due to a secret mouse experiment that was kept hidden from the public … until now.

items.title: Spanish PM's Socialists party on notice after historic defeat in stronghold

children.content: Voters in the region of Andalusia have sent the ruling Spain's Socialists yet another warning, adding to concerns Pedro Sánchez's government could suffer an electoral wipe-out in a nationwide vote.

image.altText: Pedro Sanchez on a campaign stage clapping and smiling.

items.title: Journalist in London stabbed at behest of Iran, court told

children.content: Pouria Zaratifoukolaei, who works for a Persian-language outlet critical of the Iranian government, was attacked near his home in London in 2024.

image.altText: A court sketch of two men with dark hair wearing smart attire behind persplex glass.

items.title: The dead fish waste disaster that Tasmania failed to plan for

children.content: High-volume salmon mortalities last year swamped Tasmania's waste streams as companies scrambled to find somewhere to accept them, documents show. There are now concerns the state remains unprepared for future mass deaths.

items.title: Blanchett says #MeToo reckoning in Hollywood 'got killed very quickly'

children.content: The Oscar-winning actor says Hollywood's reckoning over sexual misconduct and gender equality has stalled despite the global impact of #MeToo.

items.title: How netball's 'super' veteran left retirement to break 12-year record

children.content: Romelda Aiken-George is supposed to be retired. Instead, the 37-year-old goal shooter is breaking all kinds of records and reinventing herself in her 19th season. After her 255th national league game on the weekend, she reveals the secret to her longevity.

items.title: Horror MotoGP crash leaves rider with broken neck
